post.description:
Onboard a wellness provider.

Mirrors the manual-doctor flow but writes ``account_type='wellness'`` and
skips medical-council ID lookups (Surepass). The ``registration_no`` here
is a free-form membership id (not validated against doctor MCI numbers).
Submissions land in the same on_review queue as doctors.

Steps share the ``signin_screen_entered`` event and are distinguished by
the ``step_name`` property. ``window_hours`` is the time a user has to
move through all 4 steps from their first one.

schema.title: Response List Personal Tasks Api Internal Tool Personal Tasks Get

post.description:
Scan pending tasks with a due_date within the next 24 hours and email
each of their assignees. Designed to be called once a day by an external
scheduler — running it twice in a 24h window is safe but will send the
same recipient two reminders.

Manually fire a VoIP push at ``user_id``'s stored voip_token.

Temporary test trigger: NOT wired to the RTC / call.invite flow yet. It
exists so a VoIP push can be fired end-to-end (and the Azure Notification
Hub .voip topic config verified) before the signaling integration lands.
Authenticated so it can't be used as an open push-spam endpoint.

Manually fire an Android FCM cold-ring push at ``user_id``.

Android counterpart of /test-voip: sends the same high-priority data-only
FCM message the real call flow sends, with a throwaway call id, so the
end-to-end path (credential -> FCM -> killed app rings) can be verified
without placing a real call. Authenticated so it can't be used as an open
push-spam endpoint.

Ring a user's phone for a real call (called by healthcare-ai on call.invite).

healthcare-ai is platform-blind — it fires this once per invite regardless
of the callee's device. The backend owns the push-token table, so routing
happens here: an iOS callee (stored PushKit ``voip_token``) gets the Azure
Notification Hub VoIP push; an Android callee (FCM ``push_token`` with
platform "android") gets a high-priority data-only FCM v1 message. Both are
attempted, so a user with devices on both platforms rings everywhere; each
sender no-ops cleanly when its token type isn't stored.

Kept at the historical ``/ring-voip`` path so existing healthcare-ai
deployments need no change. Uses the caller-supplied ``call_id`` verbatim
so both call sides agree on it. Auth is a shared secret header
(X-Internal-Secret), not user JWT — see ``verify_internal_secret``.

Android-only ring: high-priority DATA-ONLY FCM v1 push to the callee.

The Android counterpart of the iOS VoIP send inside ``/ring-voip``. The
routing endpoint above already covers Android on the normal call flow;
this exists for callers that have already determined the callee is on
Android and for targeted testing of the FCM path in isolation.

Write a scheduled call's status back to the DB (called by healthcare-ai).

The RTC pod owns the call lifecycle after orchestration but has no DB
access, so without this write-back a claimed row stays 'ringing' forever.
``room_id`` is the ``sched-<uuid>`` value the claim task stamped on the row
(see ``_CLAIM_DUE_SQL`` in the scheduled_calls celery task); anything that
doesn't parse back to a row id is a 404, same as an unknown id. Terminal
statuses are final — a late write against one is rejected with 409 (see
``ScheduledCallDAO.update_status``).

No-show terminal writes ('missed'/'ended_interrupted' with ``no_show_ids``)
also fire missed-call alert pushes to those users, AFTER the status write
commits and strictly best-effort: a push failure only logs, it never rolls
back the status or turns the response into an error.

Cron endpoint to generate AI posts.
All data is fetched from DB.
AI logic is identical to Streamlit.

Cron job:
- Fetch fixed communities
- Fetch joined bot users
- Generate posts using Community Matrix logic
- Attach gifs / polls in human-like sequence
- Persist posts via DAO

API layer:
- NO ORM usage
- NO DB logic
- DAO only

Cron:
- Fetch autonomous bots
- Compute follow graph
- Persist UserFollow rows
